questions.question: Given vectors u = [1, 2, 3] and v = [4, -1, 0], calculate the dot product u ⋅ v.
expectedPoints: Dot product formula: u ⋅ v = u₁v₁ + u₂v₂ + u₃v₃.

questions.question: Let vectors a = [2, -1, 5], b = [0, 3, 1], and c = [7, -2, 4]. Determine if the vector triple product a ⋅ (b × c) is equal to (a × b) ⋅ c. Show your work and explain which property this demonstrates.
expectedPoints: Calculate the cross product b × c.
Then compute the dot product a ⋅ (b × c).
Calculate the cross product a × b.
Then compute the dot product (a × b) ⋅ c.
Compare the two resulting scalar values.
Conclude that they are equal, demonstrating the property that the scalar triple product is cyclic: a ⋅ (b × c) = b ⋅ (c × a) = c ⋅ (a × b).
